# DDx GraphQL Schema
# This schema defines all queries for the GraphQL endpoint.
# Every field is doc-commented for clarity.

# Scalar definitions
scalar DateTime
scalar JSON

# Node is the base interface for all entities in the system.
interface Node {
  id: ID!
}

# ============================================
# Core Types
# ============================================

# NodeInfo represents the ddx-server instance
type NodeInfo implements Node {
  id: ID!
  name: String!
  startedAt: DateTime!
  lastSeen: DateTime!
}

# Project represents a registered ddx project
type Project implements Node {
  id: ID!
  name: String!
  path: String!
  gitRemote: String
  registeredAt: DateTime!
  lastSeen: DateTime!
  unreachable: Boolean
  tombstonedAt: DateTime
}

# Bead represents a portable work item with metadata
type Bead implements Node {
  id: ID!
  title: String!
  status: String!
  priority: Int!
  issueType: String!
  owner: String
  createdAt: DateTime!
  createdBy: String
  updatedAt: DateTime!
  labels: [String!]
  parent: String
  description: String
  acceptance: String
  notes: String
  dependencies: [Dependency!]
}

# Dependency represents a link between two beads
type Dependency {
  issueId: String!
  dependsOnId: String!
  type: String!
  createdAt: String
  createdBy: String
  metadata: String
}

# BeadEdge represents one edge in the BeadConnection cursor connection
type BeadEdge {
  node: Bead!
  cursor: String!
}

# BeadConnection represents a relay-compatible cursor connection for beads
type BeadConnection {
  edges: [BeadEdge!]!
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  totalCount: Int!
}

# Document represents a docgraph document
type Document implements Node {
  id: ID!
  path: String!
  title: String!
  dependsOn: [String!]!
  inputs: [String!]!
  dependents: [String!]!
  review: DocumentReview
  parkingLot: Boolean!
  prompt: String
  execDef: DocumentExecDef
}

# DocumentReview holds review metadata for a document
type DocumentReview {
  selfHash: String!
  deps: JSON!
  reviewedAt: String!
}

# DocumentExecDef holds execution definition for a document
type DocumentExecDef {
  id: String
  artifactIds: [String!]
  executor: ExecutorSpec
  result: ResultSpec
  evaluation: Evaluation
  active: Boolean
  required: Boolean
  graphSource: Boolean
  createdAt: DateTime
}

# ExecutorSpec captures how a definition should be invoked
type ExecutorSpec {
  kind: String!
  command: [String!]
  cwd: String
  env: JSON
  timeoutMs: Int
}

# ResultSpec describes the structured result payload
type ResultSpec {
  metric: MetricResultSpec
}

# MetricResultSpec defines metric-shaped projection fields
type MetricResultSpec {
  unit: String
  valuePath: String
  samplesPath: String
}

# Evaluation holds optional interpretation rules
type Evaluation {
  comparison: String
  thresholds: Thresholds
}

# Thresholds captures the ratchet policy
type Thresholds {
  warnMs: Float
  ratchetMs: Float
}

# DocGraph represents the document dependency graph
type DocGraph {
  rootDir: String!
  documents: [Document!]!
  pathToId: JSON!
  dependents: JSON!
  warnings: [String!]!
}

# StaleReason represents why a document is stale
type StaleReason {
  id: ID!
  path: String!
  reasons: [String!]!
}

# Commit represents a git commit
type Commit {
  sha: String!
  shortSha: String!
  author: String!
  date: DateTime!
  subject: String!
  body: String
  beadRefs: [String!]
}

# CommitEdge represents one edge in the CommitConnection cursor connection
type CommitEdge {
  node: Commit!
  cursor: String!
}

# CommitConnection represents a relay-compatible cursor connection for commits
type CommitConnection {
  edges: [CommitEdge!]!
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  totalCount: Int!
}

# Worker represents an execute-loop worker
type Worker implements Node {
  id: ID!
  kind: String!
  state: String!
  status: String
  projectRoot: String!
  harness: String
  provider: String
  model: String
  effort: String
  once: Boolean
  pollInterval: String
  startedAt: DateTime
  finishedAt: DateTime
  error: String
  stdoutPath: String
  specPath: String
  attempts: Int
  successes: Int
  failures: Int
  currentBead: String
  lastError: String
  lastResult: WorkerExecutionResult
  currentAttempt: CurrentAttemptInfo
  recentPhases: [PhaseTransition!]
  lastAttempt: LastAttemptInfo
  landSummary: CoordinatorMetrics
}

# CurrentAttemptInfo is the in-flight attempt summary
type CurrentAttemptInfo {
  attemptId: String!
  beadId: String!
  beadTitle: String
  harness: String
  model: String
  profile: String
  phase: String!
  phaseSeq: Int!
  startedAt: DateTime!
  elapsedMs: Int!
}

# PhaseTransition is one phase-transition entry
type PhaseTransition {
  phase: String!
  ts: DateTime!
  phaseSeq: Int!
}

# LastAttemptInfo summarises the most recently completed attempt
type LastAttemptInfo {
  attemptId: String!
  beadId: String!
  phase: String!
  startedAt: DateTime!
  endedAt: DateTime!
  elapsedMs: Int!
}

# WorkerExecutionResult represents an execution result
type WorkerExecutionResult {
  beadId: String
  attemptId: String
  workerId: String
  harness: String
  provider: String
  model: String
  status: String
  detail: String
  sessionId: String
  baseRev: String
  resultRev: String
  retryAfter: String
}

# AgentSession represents an agent execution session
type AgentSession implements Node {
  id: ID!
  projectId: String!
  beadId: String
  harness: String!
  model: String!
  effort: String!
  status: String!
  startedAt: DateTime!
  endedAt: DateTime
  stdoutPath: String
  stderrPath: String
  durationMs: Int!
  cost: Float
  tokens: TokenUsage
  outcome: String
  detail: String
  resultRev: String
  baseRev: String
}

# TokenUsage represents token consumption
type TokenUsage {
  prompt: Int
  completion: Int
  total: Int
  cached: Int
}

# Persona represents an AI persona definition
type Persona implements Node {
  id: ID!
  name: String!
  roles: [String!]!
  description: String!
  tags: [String!]!
  content: String!
  filePath: String
  modTime: DateTime
}

# ExecutionRun represents an execution run
type ExecutionRun implements Node {
  id: ID!
  definitionId: String!
  artifactIds: [String!]!
  startedAt: DateTime!
  finishedAt: DateTime!
  status: String!
  exitCode: Int!
  mergeBlocking: Boolean
  agentSessionId: String
  attachments: JSON
  provenance: Provenance
  metric: MetricObservation
  stdout: String
  stderr: String
  value: Float
  unit: String
  parsed: Boolean
}

# Provenance captures host and version metadata
type Provenance {
  actor: String
  host: String
  gitRev: String
  ddxVersion: String
}

# MetricObservation records a metric-shaped observation
type MetricObservation {
  artifactId: String!
  definitionId: String!
  observedAt: DateTime!
  status: String!
  value: Float!
  unit: String
  samples: [Float!]
  comparison: ComparisonResult
}

# ComparisonResult records comparison against baseline
type ComparisonResult {
  baseline: Float!
  delta: Float!
  direction: String!
}

# CoordinatorMetrics holds land coordinator metrics
type CoordinatorMetrics {
  projectRoot: String!
  branch: String!
  lastLandTime: DateTime
  lastLandResult: String
  queueLength: Int!
  meanQueueTimeMs: Float
  lastQueueTimeMs: Float
}

# Provider represents an agent provider configuration
type Provider implements Node {
  id: ID!
  name: String!
  kind: String!
  defaultModel: String
  models: [String!]
  defaultEffort: String
  effortLevels: [String!]
  timeoutMs: Int
  permissions: [String!]
}

# PageInfo represents pagination information (relay standard)
type PageInfo {
  hasNextPage: Boolean!
  hasPreviousPage: Boolean!
  startCursor: String
  endCursor: String
}

# ============================================
# Query Root
# ============================================

# Query is the entry point for all GraphQL queries
type Query {
  # node fetches any entity by ID
  node(id: ID!): Node

  # ==========================================
  # Node & Project Queries
  # ==========================================

  # nodeInfo returns the server node information
  nodeInfo: NodeInfo!

  # projects returns all registered projects
  projects(first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String, includeUnreachable: Boolean): ProjectConnection!

  # ==========================================
  # Bead Queries
  # ==========================================

  # beads returns all beads with cursor pagination
  beads(first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String, status: String, label: String, projectID: String): BeadConnection!

  # beadsByProject returns beads for a specific project
  beadsByProject(projectID: String!, first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String, status: String, label: String): BeadConnection!

  # beadsReady returns open beads whose dependencies are closed
  beadsReady(first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String): BeadConnection!

  # beadsBlocked returns open beads with unclosed dependencies
  beadsBlocked(first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String): BeadConnection!

  # beadsStatus returns aggregate bead counts
  beadsStatus: BeadStatusCounts!

  # beadDepTree returns the dependency tree for a bead
  beadDepTree(beadID: ID!): String!

  # bead returns a single bead by ID
  bead(id: ID!): Bead

  # ==========================================
  # Document Queries
  # ==========================================

  # documents returns all library documents
  documents(first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String, type: String): DocumentConnection!

  # docGraph returns the full document dependency graph
  docGraph: DocGraph!

  # docStale returns documents that are stale
  docStale: [StaleReason!]!

  # docDeps returns the dependencies of a document
  docDeps(documentID: ID!): [String!]!

  # docDependents returns the dependents of a document
  docDependents(documentID: ID!): [String!]!

  # docHistory returns the git history for a document
  docHistory(documentID: ID!, first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String): CommitConnection!

  # docDiff returns the diff for a document
  docDiff(documentID: ID!, ref: String): String!

  # doc returns a single document by ID
  doc(id: ID!): Document

  # search searches library documents
  search(query: String!, first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String): [SearchResult!]!

  # ==========================================
  # Commit Queries
  # ==========================================

  # commits returns git commits for a project
  commits(projectID: ID!, first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String, since: String, author: String): CommitConnection!

  # ==========================================
  # Worker Queries
  # ==========================================

  # workers returns all workers
  workers(first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String): WorkerConnection!

  # workersByProject returns workers for a specific project
  workersByProject(projectID: String!, first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String): WorkerConnection!

  # worker returns a single worker by ID
  worker(id: ID!): Worker

  # workerProgress returns the progress subscription info
  workerProgress(workerID: ID!): [PhaseTransition!]!

  # ==========================================
  # Agent Session Queries
  # ==========================================

  # agentSessions returns all agent sessions
  agentSessions(first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String): AgentSessionConnection!

  # agentSession returns a single session by ID
  agentSession(id: ID!): AgentSession

  # ==========================================
  # Persona Queries
  # ==========================================

  # personas returns all personas
  personas(first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String): PersonaConnection!

  # persona returns a single persona by name
  persona(name: String!): Persona

  # personaByRole returns the persona bound to a role
  personaByRole(role: String!): Persona

  # ==========================================
  # Execution Queries
  # ==========================================

  # execDefinitions returns all execution definitions
  execDefinitions(first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String, artifactID: String): ExecutionDefinitionConnection!

  # execDefinition returns a single definition by ID
  execDefinition(id: ID!): ExecutionDefinition

  # execRuns returns execution runs
  execRuns(first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String, artifactID: String, definitionID: String): ExecutionRunConnection!

  # execRun returns a single run by ID
  execRun(id: ID!): ExecutionRun

  # execRunLog returns the log for an execution run
  execRunLog(runID: ID!): ExecutionRunLog!

  # ==========================================
  # Metrics Queries
  # ==========================================

  # coordinatorMetrics returns land coordinator metrics
  coordinatorMetrics: CoordinatorMetrics!

  # coordinatorMetricsByProject returns coordinator metrics for a project
  coordinatorMetricsByProject(projectID: ID!): CoordinatorMetrics!

  # metricsSummary returns a summary of metrics
  metricsSummary: CoordinatorMetrics

  # metricsCost returns cost metrics
  metricsCost: CoordinatorMetrics

  # metricsCycleTime returns cycle time metrics
  metricsCycleTime: CoordinatorMetrics

  # metricsRework returns rework metrics
  metricsRework: CoordinatorMetrics

  # ==========================================
  # Provider Queries
  # ==========================================

  # providers returns all providers
  providers: [Provider!]!

  # provider returns a single provider by name
  provider(name: String!): Provider
}

# Connection types for cursor-based pagination
# ============================================

# ProjectConnection represents a relay-compatible cursor connection for projects
type ProjectConnection {
  edges: [ProjectEdge!]!
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  totalCount: Int!
}

# ProjectEdge represents one edge in the ProjectConnection
type ProjectEdge {
  node: Project!
  cursor: String!
}

# BeadStatusCounts holds aggregate bead counts
type BeadStatusCounts {
  open: Int!
  closed: Int!
  blocked: Int!
  ready: Int!
  total: Int!
}

# SearchResult represents a search result
type SearchResult {
  path: String!
  type: String!
  name: String!
  snippet: String
}

# WorkerConnection represents a relay-compatible cursor connection for workers
type WorkerConnection {
  edges: [WorkerEdge!]!
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  totalCount: Int!
}

# WorkerEdge represents one edge in the WorkerConnection
type WorkerEdge {
  node: Worker!
  cursor: String!
}

# AgentSessionConnection represents a relay-compatible cursor connection for agent sessions
type AgentSessionConnection {
  edges: [AgentSessionEdge!]!
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  totalCount: Int!
}

# AgentSessionEdge represents one edge in the AgentSessionConnection
type AgentSessionEdge {
  node: AgentSession!
  cursor: String!
}

# PersonaConnection represents a relay-compatible cursor connection for personas
type PersonaConnection {
  edges: [PersonaEdge!]!
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  totalCount: Int!
}

# PersonaEdge represents one edge in the PersonaConnection
type PersonaEdge {
  node: Persona!
  cursor: String!
}

# DocumentConnection represents a relay-compatible cursor connection for documents
type DocumentConnection {
  edges: [DocumentEdge!]!
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  totalCount: Int!
}

# DocumentEdge represents one edge in the DocumentConnection
type DocumentEdge {
  node: Document!
  cursor: String!
}

# ExecutionDefinitionConnection represents a relay-compatible cursor connection for execution definitions
type ExecutionDefinitionConnection {
  edges: [ExecutionDefinitionEdge!]!
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  totalCount: Int!
}

# ExecutionDefinitionEdge represents one edge in the ExecutionDefinitionConnection
type ExecutionDefinitionEdge {
  node: ExecutionDefinition!
  cursor: String!
}

# ExecutionDefinition represents an execution definition
type ExecutionDefinition implements Node {
  id: ID!
  artifactIds: [String!]!
  executor: ExecutorSpec!
  result: ResultSpec
  evaluation: Evaluation
  active: Boolean!
  required: Boolean
  graphSource: Boolean
  createdAt: DateTime!
}

# ExecutionRunConnection represents a relay-compatible cursor connection for execution runs
type ExecutionRunConnection {
  edges: [ExecutionRunEdge!]!
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  totalCount: Int!
}

# ExecutionRunEdge represents one edge in the ExecutionRunConnection
type ExecutionRunEdge {
  node: ExecutionRun!
  cursor: String!
}

# ExecutionRunLog represents an execution run log
type ExecutionRunLog {
  stdout: String!
  stderr: String!
}

tools.name: read
tools.description: Read file contents. Use instead of cat/head/tail.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

offset.description: 0-based line offset to start reading from

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to return

tools.name: write

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

content.description: Content to write to the file

tools.name: edit
tools.description: Make targeted edits to a file using exact string replacement.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

oldText.description: Exact text to find (must be unique in the file and must not overlap with other edits)

old_string.description: Legacy: exact string to find (use edits[] instead)

tools.name: grep
tools.description: Search file contents for a regex pattern. Use instead of grep/rg shell commands.

pattern.description: Regular expression to search for

dir.description: Directory to search in (relative or absolute; defaults to working directory)

glob.description: Restrict to files whose base names match this glob (e.g. '*.go', '*.ts')

exclude_dirs.description: Override default excluded directories. By default, skips .git, .hg, .svn, node_modules, and vendor/. Set to empty array [] to search all directories.

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match (e.g. '**/*.go', 'cmd/**/main.go'). Use ** to match across directories.

dir.description: Directory to search in (relative to working directory or absolute; defaults to working directory)

# DDx GraphQL Schema
# This schema defines all queries for the GraphQL endpoint.
# Every field is doc-commented for clarity.

# Scalar definitions
scalar DateTime
scalar JSON

# Node is the base interface for all entities in the system.
interface Node {
  id: ID!
}

# ============================================
# Core Types
# ============================================

# NodeInfo represents the ddx-server instance
type NodeInfo implements Node {
  id: ID!
  name: String!
  startedAt: DateTime!
  lastSeen: DateTime!
}

# Project represents a registered ddx project
type Project implements Node {
  id: ID!
  name: String!
  path: String!
  gitRemote: String
  registeredAt: DateTime!
  lastSeen: DateTime!
  unreachable: Boolean
  tombstonedAt: DateTime
}

# Bead represents a portable work item with metadata
type Bead implements Node {
  id: ID!
  title: String!
  status: String!
  priority: Int!
  issueType: String!
  owner: String
  createdAt: DateTime!
  createdBy: String
  updatedAt: DateTime!
  labels: [String!]
  parent: String
  description: String
  acceptance: String
  notes: String
  dependencies: [Dependency!]
}

# Dependency represents a link between two beads
type Dependency {
  issueId: String!
  dependsOnId: String!
  type: String!
  createdAt: String
  createdBy: String
  metadata: String
}

# BeadEdge represents one edge in the BeadConnection cursor connection
type BeadEdge {
  node: Bead!
  cursor: String!
}

# BeadConnection represents a relay-compatible cursor connection for beads
type BeadConnection {
  edges: [BeadEdge!]!
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  totalCount: Int!
}

# Document represents a docgraph document
type Document implements Node {
  id: ID!
  path: String!
  title: String!
  dependsOn: [String!]!
  inputs: [String!]!
  dependents: [String!]!
  review: DocumentReview
  parkingLot: Boolean!
  prompt: String
  execDef: DocumentExecDef
}

# DocumentReview holds review metadata for a document
type DocumentReview {
  selfHash: String!
  deps: JSON!
  reviewedAt: String!
}

You are a coding agent. Complete tasks by reading files, editing code, executing commands, and writing new files using your available tools.

Work systematically: read first, make changes, verify, report concisely.

When given a task, prefer action over discussion. If something fails, diagnose and fix it.

# Tools
- read: Read file contents. Use instead of cat/head/tail.
- write: Create or overwrite a file with the given content. Creates parent directories as needed.
- edit: Make targeted edits to a file using exact string replacement.
- bash: Execute a shell command. Returns stdout, stderr, and exit code.
- ls: List directory contents. Use instead of ls.
- grep: Search file contents for a regex pattern. Use instead of grep/rg shell commands.
- find: Find files matching a glob pattern. Use instead of the find/ls shell commands to locate files by name.

Guidelines:
- Use tools for all file operations — don't output code as text
- Read before editing
- Verify after editing when builds or tests exist
- Stay concise
- Don't add unrequested features
- Fix errors rather than reporting them

# Project Context

Project-specific instructions and guidelines:

# DDx Agent Instructions

This repository uses DDx's built-in bead tracker for durable work management.

## Bead Policy

- Treat `.ddx/beads.jsonl` as DDx-managed data, not as a hand-edited document.
- Create beads only with `ddx bead create`.
- Modify bead metadata only with `ddx bead update`.
- Manage dependencies only with `ddx bead dep add` and `ddx bead dep remove`.
- Close work only with `ddx bead close`.
- Use `ddx bead import` and `ddx bead export` for bulk migration or interchange.
- Commit tracker mutations by default after bead commands.
- If the tracker change stands alone, make a tracker-only commit promptly.
- If it belongs to related implementation/docs work already being prepared for
  commit, fold the tracker change into that same commit instead of leaving
  `.ddx/beads.jsonl` dirty.

## Prohibited Actions

- Do not edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` manually.
- Do not add, remove, or rewrite bead rows with `apply_patch`, editors, scripts, or ad hoc JSONL manipulation.
- Do not invent bead IDs or prefixes such as `hx-*` or `ddx-*`.
- Do not treat nearby tracker entries as a naming pattern to copy.

## If The CLI Seems Insufficient

- Prefer the nearest supported `ddx bead` command.
- If the required tracker operation is not supported by `ddx bead`, stop and ask rather than editing tracker storage directly.

## Verification

- Use `ddx bead show <id>` to inspect one bead.
- Use `ddx bead list`, `ddx bead ready`, and `ddx bead status` to inspect queue state.
- Use `ddx bead --help` and `ddx bead create --help` before assuming a flag exists.

## Skill Policy

- Treat `SKILL.md` frontmatter as a strict interface, not freeform metadata.
- Published DDx skills must use top-level YAML frontmatter fields `name` and `description`.
- Use `argument-hint` only when the skill accepts a trailing positional or shorthand invocation hint.
- Do not use nested `skill:` frontmatter for DDx repo skills.
- Run `ddx skills check [path ...]` for reusable validation across repo skills and plugin skills.
- Run `make skill-schema` after editing any file under `skills/*/SKILL.md` or `cli/internal/skills/*/SKILL.md`.

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is the shared infrastructure platform for document-driven development. It provides the tools that developers and workflow systems (like HELIX) use to manage the documents AI agents consume to build software.

DDx is one layer in a three-project stack:
- **DDx** (this repo) — platform services: document library, bead tracker, agent service, personas, templates, git sync
- **HELIX** (`~/Projects/helix`) — workflow methodology: phases, gates, supervisory dispatch, bounded actions
- **Dun** (`~/Projects/dun`) — quality check runner: check discovery, execution, agent-friendly output

## Architecture

This monorepo produces three artifacts:
- **`ddx` CLI** (`/cli/`): Go binary — document library mgmt, bead tracker, agent dispatch, personas, templates, git sync
- **`ddx-server`** (planned): Web server + MCP endpoints for document and bead access
- **`ddx.github.io`** (`/website/`): Hugo promotional site

### Key Components

- `cli/` - Go CLI application source code
  - `cmd/` - Cobra command implementations
  - `internal/` - Internal packages (config, bead, persona, git, mcp, metaprompt, etc.)
  - `main.go` - Application entry point
- `website/` - Hugo site with Hextra theme
- `library/` - DDx default plugin source (installed to `.ddx/plugins/ddx/` by `ddx init`)
  - `templates/` - Project templates
  - `patterns/` - Reusable code patterns
  - `prompts/` - AI prompts and instructions
  - `personas/` - AI persona definitions
  - `mcp-servers/` - MCP server registry
- `docs/helix/` - HELIX frame artifacts (vision, PRD, feature specs)
- `scripts/` - Build and automation scripts

## Development Commands

### CLI Development (run from `/cli/` directory)

```bash
# Build and test
make build          # Build for current platform
make test           # Run Go tests
make lint           # Run golangci-lint (or go vet if not available)
make fmt            # Format Go code

# Development workflow
make all            # Clean, deps, test, build
make dev            # Development mode with file watching (requires air)
make run ARGS="..."  # Run CLI with arguments
make install        # Install locally to ~/.local/bin/ddx

# Dependencies
make deps           # Install and tidy Go modules
make update-deps    # Update all dependencies

# Multi-platform builds
make build-all      # Build for all platforms
make release        # Create release archives
```

### Project Structure Navigation

The CLI uses git subtree for managing the relationship between individual projects and the master DDx repository. The `.ddx.yml` configuration file defines:
- Repository URL and branch
- Included resources (prompts, scripts, templates, patterns)
- Template variables and overrides
- Git subtree settings

### Key Patterns

1. **Command Structure**: Each CLI command is implemented as a separate file in `cli/cmd/`
2. **Configuration Management**: Uses Viper for config file handling with YAML format
3. **Template Processing**: Variable substitution system for customizing templates
4. **Git Integration**: Built on git subtree for reliable version control and contribution workflows
5. **Cross-Platform Support**: Makefile supports building for multiple platforms (macOS, Linux, Windows)

## Architectural Principles

**CRITICAL**: The DDx CLI follows the principle of "Extensibility Through Composition" - keep the CLI core minimal and add features through library resources.

1. **Platform Services in CLI, Opinions in Workflows**:
   - CLI owns: document library, bead tracker, agent dispatch, personas, templates, git sync
   - Workflow tools own: phase enforcement, supervisory loops, methodology-specific validation
   - DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions

2. **Feature Addition Pattern**:
   - Document-type resources go in the library (personas, patterns, templates)
   - Platform capabilities go in the CLI (beads, agent service, MCP)
   - Workflow-specific logic stays in workflow tools (HELIX, etc.)

## Testing and Quality

**CRITICAL**: All tests are release-critical and must pass before committing:

```bash
# Run full test suite - all tests must pass
cd cli && go test -v ./cmd
```

Every test validates critical functionality and must pass before any release.

- Go tests are in `*_test.go` files alongside source code
- Linting uses golangci-lint (fallback to go vet)
- Code formatting with `go fmt`
- Cross-platform compatibility is maintained

### Pre-commit Checks

The project uses Lefthook for git hooks. To run pre-commit checks manually:

```bash
# Run all pre-commit checks
lefthook run pre-commit

# Or stage files and run checks
git add <files>
lefthook run pre-commit
```

Pre-commit checks include:
- Secrets detection
- Binary file prevention
- Debug statement detection
- Merge conflict detection
- DDx configuration validation
- Go linting, formatting, building, and testing

## CLI Command Overview

The CLI follows a noun-verb command structure for clarity and consistency:

**Core Commands:**
- `ddx init` - Initialize DDx in a project
- `ddx doctor` - Check installation health and diagnose issues
- `ddx upgrade` - Upgrade DDx binary to latest release version
- `ddx update` - Update toolkit resources from master repository
- `ddx contribute` - Share improvements back to community
- `ddx status` - Show version and sync status

**Bead Tracker:**
- `ddx bead create/show/update/close` - Work item CRUD
- `ddx bead list/ready/blocked/status` - Query and filter beads
- `ddx bead dep add/remove/tree` - Dependency DAG management
- `ddx bead import/export` - JSONL interchange with bd/br

**Agent Service:**
- `ddx agent run --harness=<name> --prompt <file>` - Invoke an AI agent
- `ddx agent run --quorum=majority --harnesses=a,b` - Multi-agent consensus
- `ddx agent execute-bead <id> [--from <rev>] [--no-merge]` - Run agent in isolated worktree, merge or preserve result
- `ddx agent list` - Show available harnesses
- `ddx agent doctor` - Harness health check
- `ddx agent log` - Session history

**Resource Commands:**
- `ddx prompts list/show` - AI prompts
- `ddx templates list/apply` - Project templates
- `ddx persona list/show/bind` - AI personas
- `ddx mcp list/install` - MCP servers

**Embedded Utilities:**
- `ddx jq <filter> [file...]` - Embedded jq processor (via gojq), no external jq required

## Persona System

DDX includes a persona system that provides consistent AI personalities for different roles:

- **Personas**: Reusable AI personality templates (e.g., `strict-code-reviewer`, `test-engineer-tdd`)
- **Roles**: Abstract functions that personas fulfill (e.g., `code-reviewer`, `test-engineer`)
- **Bindings**: Project-specific mappings between roles and personas in `.ddx.yml`

Personas enable consistent, high-quality AI interactions across team members and projects. Projects bind specific personas to roles. See `library/personas/` for available personas and `library/personas/README.md` for detailed documentation.

# System Instructions

**Execute ONLY what is requested:**

- **YAGNI** (You Aren't Gonna Need It): Implement only specified features. No "useful additions" or "while we're here" features.
- **KISS** (Keep It Simple, Stupid): Choose the simplest solution that meets requirements. Avoid clever code or premature optimization.
- **DOWITYTD** (Do Only What I Told You To Do): Stop when the task is complete. No extra refactoring, documentation, or improvements unless explicitly requested.

**Response Style:**
- Be concise and direct
- Skip preamble and postamble
- Provide complete information without unnecessary elaboration
- Stop immediately when the task is done

**When coding:**
- Write only code needed to pass tests
- No gold-plating or speculative features
- Follow existing patterns and conventions
- Add only requested functionality

# DDx GraphQL + SvelteKit Migration Plan — v3 (locked)

**Status**: committed. 4 stages. No more plan iteration.
**Author**: claude-sonnet-4-6 (under erik's direction)
**Date**: 2026-04-14

## Locked constraints (from user)

- **Frontend**: SvelteKit, Svelte 5 runes, `adapter-static`, **Bun** (not npm/pnpm) for package management and scripts.
- **Backend**: Go + `gqlgen`. New `/graphql` endpoint. Existing REST endpoints **stay frozen**. CLI/MCP continue to call REST unchanged. Post-epic: port CLI to GraphQL, then deprecate REST.
- **URL scheme**: `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/*` from day one. DDx already has multiple nodes; the node-ID segment is not cargo-cult.
- **Auth**: existing `isTrusted()` localhost-check model stays as-is. No new middleware. No cookies. No bearer tokens. Not in this epic.
- **Delete React entirely**, rebuild from specs. Pre-release software; no 1:1 port required. Specs are the source of truth.
- **4 stages**. Each stage lands as a checkpoint before the next is filed.
- **No more compromises on scope.** The plan addresses the user's stated goals and does not trade them away for velocity.

## Why this plan exists

Two problems with the current React+REST architecture:

1. **No end-to-end type safety.** `src/types.ts` (182 LOC, 12 interfaces) is hand-mirrored from Go structs. Drift is silent and surfaces as runtime errors.
2. **No story for scale.** REST pagination is ad-hoc or absent; the Beads page fetches ALL beads and filters client-side with minisearch. Real-time is polling except for one SSE endpoint. At 10K+ beads this model breaks.

User's non-negotiable rule: *"I'm going to be nagging you about missed edge cases forever. I don't want that."* The architecture must make edge cases compile errors, not runtime surprises.

## Target state

### Backend

- **`POST /graphql`** powered by `gqlgen` — schema-first, codegen produces typed Go resolvers.
- **`schema.graphql`** is the single source of truth. TypeScript client generated from it via Houdini.
- **Relay cursor connections** for every list type. Every page that displays a list uses cursors.
- **Subscriptions** over `graphql-ws`: `workerProgress`, `beadLifecycle`, `executionEvidence`, `coordinatorMetrics`.
- **REST + MCP untouched.** CLI keeps working. MCP tools keep their shapes. Existing `isTrusted()` enforcement stays.
- **Dataloaders**: **deferred** — current `bead.Store` is in-memory so N+1 isn't the bottleneck. Add when a persistent store lands.

### Frontend

- **SvelteKit** (Svelte 5 runes) + `adapter-static`. Builds to static files. Go serves via `//go:embed`.
- **Bun** for package management and scripts. `bun install`, `bun run dev`, `bun run build`, `bun run test`.
- **Houdini** as the GraphQL client — SvelteKit-sanctioned, codegen from `schema.graphql`, typed `load()` functions and typed subscriptions.
- **`bits-ui`** headless primitives + Tailwind styling. `lucide-svelte` icons. `mode-watcher` for dark mode.
- **Testing**: Playwright for e2e, Vitest for unit, svelte-check for typecheck.
- **URL scheme**: `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/*` — implemented fresh, not ported.

### Deployment

Unchanged. Go binary embeds SvelteKit build via `//go:embed`. Single binary. No Node runtime in production. Systemd service unchanged.

## Bead authoring rules (enforced per-bead)

Every bead must have:

1. **A user-visible acceptance step**. Not "tests pass." A human runs the server, navigates/clicks/observes a specific outcome.
2. **Explicit file list**. Every file the bead modifies.
3. **Spec-in-sync**. If the bead's acceptance contradicts any spec, the bead edits the spec in the same commit.
4. **Tests**: Go integration test (real store, real git, zero mocks) for backend beads; Vitest + Playwright e2e for frontend beads.
5. **Small enough for sonnet to finish confidently in ≤30 minutes wall-clock.** Split aggressively.

## Totals

- **Stage 1**: 13 beads (3 L, 6 M, 4 S) → ~4 hours sonnet-time
- **Stage 2**: 13 beads (1 L, 8 M, 4 S) → ~4 hours
- **Stage 3**: 8 beads (1 L, 5 M, 2 S) → ~3 hours
- **Stage 4**: 17 beads (3 L, 8 M, 6 S) → ~6 hours
- **Total**: 51 beads, ~17 hours sonnet-time. Wall-clock at execute-loop pace: 2-3 days.

## Risks

1. **Schema design is critical path.** Bad schema = rework everywhere. Beads 1-2 get a human review gate before Stage 2 files.
2. **Svelte 5 ecosystem churn.** Bead 29 verifies compatibility explicitly. Fallback is documented (downgrade to Svelte 4 for the first pass, upgrade later) — but the plan has NO other fallback for a library-level incompatibility.
3. **Subscription lifecycle bugs.** WebSocket drops, cleanup, reconnect. Bead 26's integration tests must drive a real disconnect.
4. **D3 graph page (bead 45) is the most technically sensitive.** Sized L, may need further split if the force-sim + drag + zoom code is bigger than expected.
5. **Bead 29 (Svelte 5 compat) is a gate for Stage 3.** If it finds an incompat, the whole scaffold strategy may need revisiting. Schedule early.
6. **Pre-existing `installation acceptance tests`** (known flaky) may fail during Stage 4 CI rewrite. Accept as pre-existing; do not attempt to fix inside this epic.
7. **Stage 3 bead 27 (delete React)** is destructive but safe: user confirmed the React app has only been loaded once; no production state lost.

## What this plan does NOT include

- Rewriting the CLI to use GraphQL. REST stays for the CLI.
- Rewriting MCP tools. MCP stays at `POST /mcp` with current shapes.
- Auth middleware. Existing `isTrusted()` localhost model stays as-is.
- Dataloaders. Deferred until persistent store exists.
- Observability / tracing / request IDs. Separate concern.
- Deprecating REST. Post-epic, separate effort.
- Data migration. None needed.

## Success criteria

- Every page from the original React app has an equivalent (or better) Svelte page built from FEAT-008 + FEAT-021 specs.
- Every GraphQL operation has a real-store integration test passing (zero mocks).

# DDx GraphQL Schema
# This schema defines all queries for the GraphQL endpoint.
# Every field is doc-commented for clarity.

# Scalar definitions
scalar DateTime
scalar JSON

# Node is the base interface for all entities in the system.
interface Node {
  id: ID!
}

# ============================================
# Core Types
# ============================================

# NodeInfo represents the ddx-server instance
type NodeInfo implements Node {
  id: ID!
  name: String!
  startedAt: DateTime!
  lastSeen: DateTime!
}

# Project represents a registered ddx project
type Project implements Node {
  id: ID!
  name: String!
  path: String!
  gitRemote: String
  registeredAt: DateTime!
  lastSeen: DateTime!
  unreachable: Boolean
  tombstonedAt: DateTime
}

# Bead represents a portable work item with metadata
type Bead implements Node {
  id: ID!
  title: String!
  status: String!
  priority: Int!
  issueType: String!
  owner: String
  createdAt: DateTime!
  createdBy: String
  updatedAt: DateTime!
  labels: [String!]
  parent: String
  description: String
  acceptance: String
  notes: String
  dependencies: [Dependency!]
}

# Dependency represents a link between two beads
type Dependency {
  issueId: String!
  dependsOnId: String!
  type: String!
  createdAt: String
  createdBy: String
  metadata: String
}

# BeadEdge represents one edge in the BeadConnection cursor connection
type BeadEdge {
  node: Bead!
  cursor: String!
}

# BeadConnection represents a relay-compatible cursor connection for beads
type BeadConnection {
  edges: [BeadEdge!]!
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  totalCount: Int!
}

# Document represents a docgraph document
type Document implements Node {
  id: ID!
  path: String!
  title: String!
  dependsOn: [String!]!
  inputs: [String!]!
  dependents: [String!]!
  review: DocumentReview
  parkingLot: Boolean!
  prompt: String
  execDef: DocumentExecDef
}

# DocumentReview holds review metadata for a document
type DocumentReview {
  selfHash: String!
  deps: JSON!
  reviewedAt: String!
}

# DocumentExecDef holds execution definition for a document
type DocumentExecDef {
  id: String
  artifactIds: [String!]
  executor: ExecutorSpec
  result: ResultSpec
  evaluation: Evaluation
  active: Boolean
  required: Boolean
  graphSource: Boolean
  createdAt: DateTime
}

# ExecutorSpec captures how a definition should be invoked
type ExecutorSpec {
  kind: String!
  command: [String!]
  cwd: String
  env: JSON
  timeoutMs: Int
}

# ResultSpec describes the structured result payload
type ResultSpec {
  metric: MetricResultSpec
}

# MetricResultSpec defines metric-shaped projection fields
type MetricResultSpec {
  unit: String
  valuePath: String
  samplesPath: String
}

# Evaluation holds optional interpretation rules
type Evaluation {
  comparison: String
  thresholds: Thresholds
}

# Thresholds captures the ratchet policy
type Thresholds {
  warnMs: Float
  ratchetMs: Float
}

# DocGraph represents the document dependency graph
type DocGraph {
  rootDir: String!
  documents: [Document!]!
  pathToId: JSON!
  dependents: JSON!
  warnings: [String!]!
}

# StaleReason represents why a document is stale
type StaleReason {
  id: ID!
  path: String!
  reasons: [String!]!
}

# Commit represents a git commit
type Commit {
  sha: String!
  shortSha: String!
  author: String!
  date: DateTime!
  subject: String!
  body: String
  beadRefs: [String!]
}

# CommitEdge represents one edge in the CommitConnection cursor connection
type CommitEdge {
  node: Commit!
  cursor: String!
}

# CommitConnection represents a relay-compatible cursor connection for commits
type CommitConnection {
  edges: [CommitEdge!]!
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  totalCount: Int!
}

# Worker represents an execute-loop worker
type Worker implements Node {
  id: ID!
  kind: String!
  state: String!
  status: String
  projectRoot: String!
  harness: String
  provider: String
  model: String
  effort: String
  once: Boolean
  pollInterval: String
  startedAt: DateTime
  finishedAt: DateTime
  error: String
  stdoutPath: String
  specPath: String
  attempts: Int
  successes: Int
  failures: Int
  currentBead: String
  lastError: String
  lastResult: WorkerExecutionResult
  currentAttempt: CurrentAttemptInfo
  recentPhases: [PhaseTransition!]
  lastAttempt: LastAttemptInfo
  landSummary: CoordinatorMetrics
}

# CurrentAttemptInfo is the in-flight attempt summary
type CurrentAttemptInfo {
  attemptId: String!
  beadId: String!
  beadTitle: String
  harness: String
  model: String
  profile: String
  phase: String!
  phaseSeq: Int!
  startedAt: DateTime!
  elapsedMs: Int!
}

# PhaseTransition is one phase-transition entry
type PhaseTransition {
  phase: String!
  ts: DateTime!
  phaseSeq: Int!
}

# LastAttemptInfo summarises the most recently completed attempt
type LastAttemptInfo {
  attemptId: String!
  beadId: String!
  phase: String!
  startedAt: DateTime!
  endedAt: DateTime!
  elapsedMs: Int!
}

# WorkerExecutionResult represents an execution result
type WorkerExecutionResult {
  beadId: String
  attemptId: String
  workerId: String
  harness: String
  provider: String
  model: String
  status: String
  detail: String
  sessionId: String
  baseRev: String
  resultRev: String
  retryAfter: String
}

# AgentSession represents an agent execution session
type AgentSession implements Node {
  id: ID!
  projectId: String!
  beadId: String
  harness: String!
  model: String!
  effort: String!
  status: String!
  startedAt: DateTime!
  endedAt: DateTime
  stdoutPath: String
  stderrPath: String
  durationMs: Int!
  cost: Float
  tokens: TokenUsage
  outcome: String
  detail: String
  resultRev: String
  baseRev: String
}

# TokenUsage represents token consumption
type TokenUsage {
  prompt: Int
  completion: Int
  total: Int
  cached: Int
}

# Persona represents an AI persona definition
type Persona implements Node {
  id: ID!
  name: String!
  roles: [String!]!
  description: String!
  tags: [String!]!
  content: String!
  filePath: String
  modTime: DateTime
}

# ExecutionRun represents an execution run
type ExecutionRun implements Node {
  id: ID!
  definitionId: String!
  artifactIds: [String!]!
  startedAt: DateTime!
  finishedAt: DateTime!
  status: String!
  exitCode: Int!
  mergeBlocking: Boolean
  agentSessionId: String
  attachments: JSON
  provenance: Provenance
  metric: MetricObservation
  stdout: String
  stderr: String
  value: Float
  unit: String
  parsed: Boolean
}

# Provenance captures host and version metadata
type Provenance {
  actor: String
  host: String
  gitRev: String
  ddxVersion: String
}

# MetricObservation records a metric-shaped observation
type MetricObservation {
  artifactId: String!
  definitionId: String!
  observedAt: DateTime!
  status: String!
  value: Float!
  unit: String
  samples: [Float!]
  comparison: ComparisonResult
}

# ComparisonResult records comparison against baseline
type ComparisonResult {
  baseline: Float!
  delta: Float!
  direction: String!
}

# CoordinatorMetrics holds land coordinator metrics
type CoordinatorMetrics {
  projectRoot: String!
  branch: String!
  lastLandTime: DateTime
  lastLandResult: String
  queueLength: Int!
  meanQueueTimeMs: Float
  lastQueueTimeMs: Float
}

# Provider represents an agent provider configuration
type Provider implements Node {
  id: ID!
  name: String!
  kind: String!
  defaultModel: String
  models: [String!]
  defaultEffort: String
  effortLevels: [String!]
  timeoutMs: Int
  permissions: [String!]
}

# PageInfo represents pagination information (relay standard)
type PageInfo {
  hasNextPage: Boolean!
  hasPreviousPage: Boolean!
  startCursor: String
  endCursor: String
}

# ============================================
# Query Root
# ============================================

# Query is the entry point for all GraphQL queries
type Query {
  # node fetches any entity by ID
  node(id: ID!): Node

  # ==========================================
  # Node & Project Queries
  # ==========================================

  # nodeInfo returns the server node information
  nodeInfo: NodeInfo!

  # projects returns all registered projects
  projects(first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String, includeUnreachable: Boolean): ProjectConnection!

  # ==========================================
  # Bead Queries
  # ==========================================

  # beads returns all beads with cursor pagination
  beads(first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String, status: String, label: String, projectID: String): BeadConnection!

  # beadsByProject returns beads for a specific project
  beadsByProject(projectID: String!, first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String, status: String, label: String): BeadConnection!

  # beadsReady returns open beads whose dependencies are closed
  beadsReady(first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String): BeadConnection!

  # beadsBlocked returns open beads with unclosed dependencies
  beadsBlocked(first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String): BeadConnection!

  # beadsStatus returns aggregate bead counts
  beadsStatus: BeadStatusCounts!

  # beadDepTree returns the dependency tree for a bead
  beadDepTree(beadID: ID!): String!

  # bead returns a single bead by ID
  bead(id: ID!): Bead

  # ==========================================
  # Document Queries
  # ==========================================

  # documents returns all library documents
  documents(first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String, type: String): DocumentConnection!

  # docGraph returns the full document dependency graph
  docGraph: DocGraph!

  # docStale returns documents that are stale
  docStale: [StaleReason!]!

  # docDeps returns the dependencies of a document
  docDeps(documentID: ID!): [String!]!

  # docDependents returns the dependents of a document
  docDependents(documentID: ID!): [String!]!

  # docHistory returns the git history for a document
  docHistory(documentID: ID!, first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String): CommitConnection!

  # docDiff returns the diff for a document
  docDiff(documentID: ID!, ref: String): String!

  # doc returns a single document by ID
  doc(id: ID!): Document

  # search searches library documents
  search(query: String!, first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String): [SearchResult!]!

  # ==========================================
  # Commit Queries
  # ==========================================

  # commits returns git commits for a project
  commits(projectID: ID!, first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String, since: String, author: String): CommitConnection!

  # ==========================================
  # Worker Queries
  # ==========================================

  # workers returns all workers
  workers(first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String): WorkerConnection!

  # workersByProject returns workers for a specific project
  workersByProject(projectID: String!, first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String): WorkerConnection!

  # worker returns a single worker by ID
  worker(id: ID!): Worker

  # workerProgress returns the progress subscription info
  workerProgress(workerID: ID!): [PhaseTransition!]!

  # ==========================================
  # Agent Session Queries
  # ==========================================

  # agentSessions returns all agent sessions
  agentSessions(first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String): AgentSessionConnection!

  # agentSession returns a single session by ID
  agentSession(id: ID!): AgentSession

  # ==========================================
  # Persona Queries
  # ==========================================

  # personas returns all personas
  personas(first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String): PersonaConnection!

  # persona returns a single persona by name
  persona(name: String!): Persona

  # personaByRole returns the persona bound to a role
  personaByRole(role: String!): Persona

  # ==========================================
  # Execution Queries
  # ==========================================

  # execDefinitions returns all execution definitions
  execDefinitions(first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String, artifactID: String): ExecutionDefinitionConnection!

  # execDefinition returns a single definition by ID
  execDefinition(id: ID!): ExecutionDefinition

  # execRuns returns execution runs
  execRuns(first: Int, after: String, last: Int, before: String, artifactID: String, definitionID: String): ExecutionRunConnection!

  # execRun returns a single run by ID
  execRun(id: ID!): ExecutionRun

  # execRunLog returns the log for an execution run
  execRunLog(runID: ID!): ExecutionRunLog!

  # ==========================================
  # Metrics Queries
  # ==========================================

  # coordinatorMetrics returns land coordinator metrics
  coordinatorMetrics: CoordinatorMetrics!

  # coordinatorMetricsByProject returns coordinator metrics for a project
  coordinatorMetricsByProject(projectID: ID!): CoordinatorMetrics!

  # metricsSummary returns a summary of metrics
  metricsSummary: CoordinatorMetrics

  # metricsCost returns cost metrics
  metricsCost: CoordinatorMetrics

  # metricsCycleTime returns cycle time metrics
  metricsCycleTime: CoordinatorMetrics

  # metricsRework returns rework metrics
  metricsRework: CoordinatorMetrics

  # ==========================================
  # Provider Queries
  # ==========================================

  # providers returns all providers
  providers: [Provider!]!

  # provider returns a single provider by name
  provider(name: String!): Provider
}

# Connection types for cursor-based pagination
# ============================================

# ProjectConnection represents a relay-compatible cursor connection for projects
type ProjectConnection {
  edges: [ProjectEdge!]!
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  totalCount: Int!
}

# ProjectEdge represents one edge in the ProjectConnection
type ProjectEdge {
  node: Project!
  cursor: String!
}

# BeadStatusCounts holds aggregate bead counts
type BeadStatusCounts {
  open: Int!
  closed: Int!
  blocked: Int!
  ready: Int!
  total: Int!
}

# SearchResult represents a search result
type SearchResult {
  path: String!
  type: String!
  name: String!
  snippet: String
}

# WorkerConnection represents a relay-compatible cursor connection for workers
type WorkerConnection {
  edges: [WorkerEdge!]!
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  totalCount: Int!
}

# WorkerEdge represents one edge in the WorkerConnection
type WorkerEdge {
  node: Worker!
  cursor: String!
}

# AgentSessionConnection represents a relay-compatible cursor connection for agent sessions
type AgentSessionConnection {
  edges: [AgentSessionEdge!]!
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  totalCount: Int!
}

# AgentSessionEdge represents one edge in the AgentSessionConnection
type AgentSessionEdge {
  node: AgentSession!
  cursor: String!
}

# PersonaConnection represents a relay-compatible cursor connection for personas
type PersonaConnection {
  edges: [PersonaEdge!]!
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  totalCount: Int!
}

# PersonaEdge represents one edge in the PersonaConnection
type PersonaEdge {
  node: Persona!
  cursor: String!
}

# DocumentConnection represents a relay-compatible cursor connection for documents
type DocumentConnection {
  edges: [DocumentEdge!]!
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  totalCount: Int!
}

# DocumentEdge represents one edge in the DocumentConnection
type DocumentEdge {
  node: Document!
  cursor: String!
}

# ExecutionDefinitionConnection represents a relay-compatible cursor connection for execution definitions
type ExecutionDefinitionConnection {
  edges: [ExecutionDefinitionEdge!]!
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  totalCount: Int!
}

# ExecutionDefinitionEdge represents one edge in the ExecutionDefinitionConnection
type ExecutionDefinitionEdge {
  node: ExecutionDefinition!
  cursor: String!
}

# ExecutionDefinition represents an execution definition
type ExecutionDefinition implements Node {
  id: ID!
  artifactIds: [String!]!
  executor: ExecutorSpec!
  result: ResultSpec
  evaluation: Evaluation
  active: Boolean!
  required: Boolean
  graphSource: Boolean
  createdAt: DateTime!
}

# ExecutionRunConnection represents a relay-compatible cursor connection for execution runs
type ExecutionRunConnection {
  edges: [ExecutionRunEdge!]!
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  totalCount: Int!
}

# ExecutionRunEdge represents one edge in the ExecutionRunConnection
type ExecutionRunEdge {
  node: ExecutionRun!
  cursor: String!
}

# ExecutionRunLog represents an execution run log
type ExecutionRunLog {
  stdout: String!
  stderr: String!
}

# DDx GraphQL Schema
# This schema defines all queries for the GraphQL endpoint.
# Every field is doc-commented for clarity.

# Scalar definitions
scalar DateTime
scalar JSON

# Node is the base interface for all entities in the system.
interface Node {
  id: ID!
}

# ============================================
# Core Types
# ============================================

# NodeInfo represents the ddx-server instance
type NodeInfo implements Node {
  id: ID!
  name: String!
  startedAt: DateTime!
  lastSeen: DateTime!
}

# Project represents a registered ddx project
type Project implements Node {
  id: ID!
  name: String!
  path: String!
  gitRemote: String
  registeredAt: DateTime!
  lastSeen: DateTime!
  unreachable: Boolean
  tombstonedAt: DateTime
}

# Bead represents a portable work item with metadata
type Bead implements Node {
  id: ID!
  title: String!
  status: String!
  priority: Int!
  issueType: String!
  owner: String
  createdAt: DateTime!
  createdBy: String
  updatedAt: DateTime!
  labels: [String!]
  parent: String
  description: String
  acceptance: String
  notes: String
  dependencies: [Dependency!]
}

# Dependency represents a link between two beads
type Dependency {
  issueId: String!
  dependsOnId: String!
  type: String!
  createdAt: String
  createdBy: String
  metadata: String
}

# BeadEdge represents one edge in the BeadConnection cursor connection
type BeadEdge {
  node: Bead!
  cursor: String!
}

# BeadConnection represents a relay-compatible cursor connection for beads
type BeadConnection {
  edges: [BeadEdge!]!
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  totalCount: Int!
}

# Document represents a docgraph document
type Document implements Node {
  id: ID!
  path: String!
  title: String!
  dependsOn: [String!]!
  inputs: [String!]!
  dependents: [String!]!
  review: DocumentReview
  parkingLot: Boolean!
  prompt: String
  execDef: DocumentExecDef
}

# DocumentReview holds review metadata for a document
type DocumentReview {
  selfHash: String!
  deps: JSON!
  reviewedAt: String!
}

# DDx Agent Instructions

This repository uses DDx's built-in bead tracker for durable work management.

## Bead Policy

- Treat `.ddx/beads.jsonl` as DDx-managed data, not as a hand-edited document.
- Create beads only with `ddx bead create`.
- Modify bead metadata only with `ddx bead update`.
- Manage dependencies only with `ddx bead dep add` and `ddx bead dep remove`.
- Close work only with `ddx bead close`.
- Use `ddx bead import` and `ddx bead export` for bulk migration or interchange.
- Commit tracker mutations by default after bead commands.
- If the tracker change stands alone, make a tracker-only commit promptly.
- If it belongs to related implementation/docs work already being prepared for
  commit, fold the tracker change into that same commit instead of leaving
  `.ddx/beads.jsonl` dirty.

## Prohibited Actions

- Do not edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` manually.
- Do not add, remove, or rewrite bead rows with `apply_patch`, editors, scripts, or ad hoc JSONL manipulation.
- Do not invent bead IDs or prefixes such as `hx-*` or `ddx-*`.
- Do not treat nearby tracker entries as a naming pattern to copy.

## If The CLI Seems Insufficient

- Prefer the nearest supported `ddx bead` command.
- If the required tracker operation is not supported by `ddx bead`, stop and ask rather than editing tracker storage directly.

## Verification

- Use `ddx bead show <id>` to inspect one bead.
- Use `ddx bead list`, `ddx bead ready`, and `ddx bead status` to inspect queue state.
- Use `ddx bead --help` and `ddx bead create --help` before assuming a flag exists.

## Skill Policy

- Treat `SKILL.md` frontmatter as a strict interface, not freeform metadata.
- Published DDx skills must use top-level YAML frontmatter fields `name` and `description`.
- Use `argument-hint` only when the skill accepts a trailing positional or shorthand invocation hint.
- Do not use nested `skill:` frontmatter for DDx repo skills.
- Run `ddx skills check [path ...]` for reusable validation across repo skills and plugin skills.
- Run `make skill-schema` after editing any file under `skills/*/SKILL.md` or `cli/internal/skills/*/SKILL.md`.


## /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-fe5c64aa-20260414T192339-18ff581e/CLAUDE.md

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is the shared infrastructure platform for document-driven development. It provides the tools that developers and workflow systems (like HELIX) use to manage the documents AI agents consume to build software.

DDx is one layer in a three-project stack:
- **DDx** (this repo) — platform services: document library, bead tracker, agent service, personas, templates, git sync
- **HELIX** (`~/Projects/helix`) — workflow methodology: phases, gates, supervisory dispatch, bounded actions
- **Dun** (`~/Projects/dun`) — quality check runner: check discovery, execution, agent-friendly output

## Architecture

This monorepo produces three artifacts:
- **`ddx` CLI** (`/cli/`): Go binary — document library mgmt, bead tracker, agent dispatch, personas, templates, git sync
- **`ddx-server`** (planned): Web server + MCP endpoints for document and bead access
- **`ddx.github.io`** (`/website/`): Hugo promotional site

### Key Components

- `cli/` - Go CLI application source code
  - `cmd/` - Cobra command implementations
  - `internal/` - Internal packages (config, bead, persona, git, mcp, metaprompt, etc.)
  - `main.go` - Application entry point
- `website/` - Hugo site with Hextra theme
- `library/` - DDx default plugin source (installed to `.ddx/plugins/ddx/` by `ddx init`)
  - `templates/` - Project templates
  - `patterns/` - Reusable code patterns
  - `prompts/` - AI prompts and instructions
  - `personas/` - AI persona definitions
  - `mcp-servers/` - MCP server registry
- `docs/helix/` - HELIX frame artifacts (vision, PRD, feature specs)
- `scripts/` - Build and automation scripts

## Development Commands

### CLI Development (run from `/cli/` directory)

```bash
# Build and test
make build          # Build for current platform
make test           # Run Go tests
make lint           # Run golangci-lint (or go vet if not available)
make fmt            # Format Go code

# Development workflow
make all            # Clean, deps, test, build
make dev            # Development mode with file watching (requires air)
make run ARGS="..."  # Run CLI with arguments
make install        # Install locally to ~/.local/bin/ddx

# Dependencies
make deps           # Install and tidy Go modules
make update-deps    # Update all dependencies

# Multi-platform builds
make build-all      # Build for all platforms
make release        # Create release archives
```

### Project Structure Navigation

The CLI uses git subtree for managing the relationship between individual projects and the master DDx repository. The `.ddx.yml` configuration file defines:
- Repository URL and branch
- Included resources (prompts, scripts, templates, patterns)
- Template variables and overrides
- Git subtree settings

### Key Patterns

1. **Command Structure**: Each CLI command is implemented as a separate file in `cli/cmd/`
2. **Configuration Management**: Uses Viper for config file handling with YAML format
3. **Template Processing**: Variable substitution system for customizing templates
4. **Git Integration**: Built on git subtree for reliable version control and contribution workflows
5. **Cross-Platform Support**: Makefile supports building for multiple platforms (macOS, Linux, Windows)

## Architectural Principles

**CRITICAL**: The DDx CLI follows the principle of "Extensibility Through Composition" - keep the CLI core minimal and add features through library resources.

1. **Platform Services in CLI, Opinions in Workflows**:
   - CLI owns: document library, bead tracker, agent dispatch, personas, templates, git sync
   - Workflow tools own: phase enforcement, supervisory loops, methodology-specific validation
   - DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions

2. **Feature Addition Pattern**:
   - Document-type resources go in the library (personas, patterns, templates)
   - Platform capabilities go in the CLI (beads, agent service, MCP)
   - Workflow-specific logic stays in workflow tools (HELIX, etc.)

## Testing and Quality

**CRITICAL**: All tests are release-critical and must pass before committing:

```bash
# Run full test suite - all tests must pass
cd cli && go test -v ./cmd
```

Every test validates critical functionality and must pass before any release.

- Go tests are in `*_test.go` files alongside source code
- Linting uses golangci-lint (fallback to go vet)
- Code formatting with `go fmt`
- Cross-platform compatibility is maintained

### Pre-commit Checks

The project uses Lefthook for git hooks. To run pre-commit checks manually:

```bash
# Run all pre-commit checks
lefthook run pre-commit

# Or stage files and run checks
git add <files>
lefthook run pre-commit
```

Pre-commit checks include:
- Secrets detection
- Binary file prevention
- Debug statement detection
- Merge conflict detection
- DDx configuration validation
- Go linting, formatting, building, and testing

## CLI Command Overview

The CLI follows a noun-verb command structure for clarity and consistency:

**Core Commands:**
- `ddx init` - Initialize DDx in a project
- `ddx doctor` - Check installation health and diagnose issues
- `ddx upgrade` - Upgrade DDx binary to latest release version
- `ddx update` - Update toolkit resources from master repository
- `ddx contribute` - Share improvements back to community
- `ddx status` - Show version and sync status

**Bead Tracker:**
- `ddx bead create/show/update/close` - Work item CRUD
- `ddx bead list/ready/blocked/status` - Query and filter beads
- `ddx bead dep add/remove/tree` - Dependency DAG management
- `ddx bead import/export` - JSONL interchange with bd/br

**Agent Service:**
- `ddx agent run --harness=<name> --prompt <file>` - Invoke an AI agent
- `ddx agent run --quorum=majority --harnesses=a,b` - Multi-agent consensus
- `ddx agent execute-bead <id> [--from <rev>] [--no-merge]` - Run agent in isolated worktree, merge or preserve result
- `ddx agent list` - Show available harnesses
- `ddx agent doctor` - Harness health check
- `ddx agent log` - Session history

**Resource Commands:**
- `ddx prompts list/show` - AI prompts
- `ddx templates list/apply` - Project templates
- `ddx persona list/show/bind` - AI personas
- `ddx mcp list/install` - MCP servers

**Embedded Utilities:**
- `ddx jq <filter> [file...]` - Embedded jq processor (via gojq), no external jq required

## Persona System

DDX includes a persona system that provides consistent AI personalities for different roles:

- **Personas**: Reusable AI personality templates (e.g., `strict-code-reviewer`, `test-engineer-tdd`)
- **Roles**: Abstract functions that personas fulfill (e.g., `code-reviewer`, `test-engineer`)
- **Bindings**: Project-specific mappings between roles and personas in `.ddx.yml`

Personas enable consistent, high-quality AI interactions across team members and projects. Projects bind specific personas to roles. See `library/personas/` for available personas and `library/personas/README.md` for detailed documentation.

# System Instructions

**Execute ONLY what is requested:**

- **YAGNI** (You Aren't Gonna Need It): Implement only specified features. No "useful additions" or "while we're here" features.
- **KISS** (Keep It Simple, Stupid): Choose the simplest solution that meets requirements. Avoid clever code or premature optimization.
- **DOWITYTD** (Do Only What I Told You To Do): Stop when the task is complete. No extra refactoring, documentation, or improvements unless explicitly requested.

**Response Style:**
- Be concise and direct
- Skip preamble and postamble
- Provide complete information without unnecessary elaboration
- Stop immediately when the task is done

**When coding:**
- Write only code needed to pass tests
- No gold-plating or speculative features
- Follow existing patterns and conventions
- Add only requested functionality

	s.mux.HandleFunc("GET /api/providers", s.handleListProviders)
	s.mux.HandleFunc("GET /api/providers/{harness}", s.handleShowProvider)

	// MCP
	s.mux.HandleFunc("POST /mcp", s.handleMCP)

	// Web UI (embedded SPA)
	distFS, err := fs.Sub(frontendFiles, "frontend/dist")
	if err == nil {
		s.mux.Handle("/", spaHandler(http.FS(distFS)))
	}
}

# DDx GraphQL + SvelteKit Migration Plan — v3 (locked)

**Status**: committed. 4 stages. No more plan iteration.
**Author**: claude-sonnet-4-6 (under erik's direction)
**Date**: 2026-04-14

## Locked constraints (from user)

- **Frontend**: SvelteKit, Svelte 5 runes, `adapter-static`, **Bun** (not npm/pnpm) for package management and scripts.
- **Backend**: Go + `gqlgen`. New `/graphql` endpoint. Existing REST endpoints **stay frozen**. CLI/MCP continue to call REST unchanged. Post-epic: port CLI to GraphQL, then deprecate REST.
- **URL scheme**: `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/*` from day one. DDx already has multiple nodes; the node-ID segment is not cargo-cult.
- **Auth**: existing `isTrusted()` localhost-check model stays as-is. No new middleware. No cookies. No bearer tokens. Not in this epic.
- **Delete React entirely**, rebuild from specs. Pre-release software; no 1:1 port required. Specs are the source of truth.
- **4 stages**. Each stage lands as a checkpoint before the next is filed.
- **No more compromises on scope.** The plan addresses the user's stated goals and does not trade them away for velocity.

## Why this plan exists

Two problems with the current React+REST architecture:

1. **No end-to-end type safety.** `src/types.ts` (182 LOC, 12 interfaces) is hand-mirrored from Go structs. Drift is silent and surfaces as runtime errors.
2. **No story for scale.** REST pagination is ad-hoc or absent; the Beads page fetches ALL beads and filters client-side with minisearch. Real-time is polling except for one SSE endpoint. At 10K+ beads this model breaks.

User's non-negotiable rule: *"I'm going to be nagging you about missed edge cases forever. I don't want that."* The architecture must make edge cases compile errors, not runtime surprises.

## Target state

### Backend

- **`POST /graphql`** powered by `gqlgen` — schema-first, codegen produces typed Go resolvers.
- **`schema.graphql`** is the single source of truth. TypeScript client generated from it via Houdini.
- **Relay cursor connections** for every list type. Every page that displays a list uses cursors.
- **Subscriptions** over `graphql-ws`: `workerProgress`, `beadLifecycle`, `executionEvidence`, `coordinatorMetrics`.
- **REST + MCP untouched.** CLI keeps working. MCP tools keep their shapes. Existing `isTrusted()` enforcement stays.
- **Dataloaders**: **deferred** — current `bead.Store` is in-memory so N+1 isn't the bottleneck. Add when a persistent store lands.

### Frontend

- **SvelteKit** (Svelte 5 runes) + `adapter-static`. Builds to static files. Go serves via `//go:embed`.
- **Bun** for package management and scripts. `bun install`, `bun run dev`, `bun run build`, `bun run test`.
- **Houdini** as the GraphQL client — SvelteKit-sanctioned, codegen from `schema.graphql`, typed `load()` functions and typed subscriptions.
- **`bits-ui`** headless primitives + Tailwind styling. `lucide-svelte` icons. `mode-watcher` for dark mode.
- **Testing**: Playwright for e2e, Vitest for unit, svelte-check for typecheck.
- **URL scheme**: `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/*` — implemented fresh, not ported.

### Deployment

Unchanged. Go binary embeds SvelteKit build via `//go:embed`. Single binary. No Node runtime in production. Systemd service unchanged.

## Bead authoring rules (enforced per-bead)

Every bead must have:

1. **A user-visible acceptance step**. Not "tests pass." A human runs the server, navigates/clicks/observes a specific outcome.
2. **Explicit file list**. Every file the bead modifies.
3. **Spec-in-sync**. If the bead's acceptance contradicts any spec, the bead edits the spec in the same commit.
4. **Tests**: Go integration test (real store, real git, zero mocks) for backend beads; Vitest + Playwright e2e for frontend beads.
5. **Small enough for sonnet to finish confidently in ≤30 minutes wall-clock.** Split aggressively.

**Stage 4 gate**: user manually walks every page, confirms everything works, green-lights the release.

## Totals

- **Stage 1**: 13 beads (3 L, 6 M, 4 S) → ~4 hours sonnet-time
- **Stage 2**: 13 beads (1 L, 8 M, 4 S) → ~4 hours
- **Stage 3**: 8 beads (1 L, 5 M, 2 S) → ~3 hours
- **Stage 4**: 17 beads (3 L, 8 M, 6 S) → ~6 hours
- **Total**: 51 beads, ~17 hours sonnet-time. Wall-clock at execute-loop pace: 2-3 days.

## Risks

1. **Schema design is critical path.** Bad schema = rework everywhere. Beads 1-2 get a human review gate before Stage 2 files.
2. **Svelte 5 ecosystem churn.** Bead 29 verifies compatibility explicitly. Fallback is documented (downgrade to Svelte 4 for the first pass, upgrade later) — but the plan has NO other fallback for a library-level incompatibility.
3. **Subscription lifecycle bugs.** WebSocket drops, cleanup, reconnect. Bead 26's integration tests must drive a real disconnect.
4. **D3 graph page (bead 45) is the most technically sensitive.** Sized L, may need further split if the force-sim + drag + zoom code is bigger than expected.
5. **Bead 29 (Svelte 5 compat) is a gate for Stage 3.** If it finds an incompat, the whole scaffold strategy may need revisiting. Schedule early.
6. **Pre-existing `installation acceptance tests`** (known flaky) may fail during Stage 4 CI rewrite. Accept as pre-existing; do not attempt to fix inside this epic.
7. **Stage 3 bead 27 (delete React)** is destructive but safe: user confirmed the React app has only been loaded once; no production state lost.

## What this plan does NOT include

- Rewriting the CLI to use GraphQL. REST stays for the CLI.
- Rewriting MCP tools. MCP stays at `POST /mcp` with current shapes.
- Auth middleware. Existing `isTrusted()` localhost model stays as-is.
- Dataloaders. Deferred until persistent store exists.
- Observability / tracing / request IDs. Separate concern.
- Deprecating REST. Post-epic, separate effort.
- Data migration. None needed.

## Success criteria

- Every page from the original React app has an equivalent (or better) Svelte page built from FEAT-008 + FEAT-021 specs.
- Every GraphQL operation has a real-store integration test passing (zero mocks).
- Playwright e2e suite passes against the new binary.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/` contains only SvelteKit source. No React files anywhere in the repo (outside of historical git blobs).
- `grep -riE "react\|tanstack" docs/ library/ website/ CLAUDE.md README.md cli/cmd/CLAUDE.md` returns only historical/superseded citations.
- `v0.6.0-alpha20` released, server restarted, a new bead lands successfully via the new UI stack.
- User reports: "I don't feel the need to nag about missed edge cases anymore."
